User Guide: C3 Electoral Register Initial Upload

01-Mar-2026

Colin Boyle (Director)
Paul Chandler (User Support)
TTW Digital Limited

©TTW Digital Limited 2025
1
TTW Digital

# Context

## Electoral Register Format

The content of an Electoral Register is defined by legislation, but the precise format is not so defined. This means that

- Each record (row) combines details of a single Elector and their Address
- The precise fields (columns) used varies by Council
- Address data is the most affected by this, and so we have developed an algorithm to interpret the various address data formats, however it is not able to achieve 100% accuracy

## Pre-Existing Canvassing Data

Many parties have gathered significant amount of canvassing data, in both previous and current election cycles - if this is the case for your party, then

- Onboarding to TTW Digital - Guide for Local Parties discusses the choices around how to get pre-existing canvassing data into the system
- This User Guide is predicated on you having selected a migration approach that uses the Electoral Register data upload

## Scope of this User Guide

This guide sets out the specific steps you need to take to

- Prepare the data file
- Upload the data file

©TTW Digital Limited 2025

TTW Digital

# Segmenting the Electoral Register

©TTW Digital Limited 2025

3

TTW Digital

# Segmenting the Electoral Register

In principle, the Electoral Register can be uploaded in a single file, however this is subject to the following considerations and constraints

## Ongoing Data Management

When you upload an Electoral Register Incremental (Monthly) update, you will need to manually resolve all ambiguous changes, so the greater the proportion of the Electoral Register you have uploaded, the more work that will be needed to maintain the data.

## File size limit

The system supports files of up to 100,000 electors.

If you wish to load up more than this, you will need to create multiple files.

## Incremental Upload

If you decide to upload a subset of the Electoral Register, you must ensure that you always upload in full each Polling District included in the subset.

## Recommendation

We recommend only uploading the electoral register for wards that you intend to be active in, adding the Electoral Register incrementally as required for the Polling Districts in Wards as and when they become active.

©TTW Digital Limited 2025

TTW Digital

# Data Preparation - Electoral Register

©TTW Digital Limited 2025

5

TTW Digital

# Electoral Register fields (1) Voter

The Data File can have the following fields for each Elector

|  Data File Column | Definition | Required?  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  Elector No. Prefix | Elector Number Prefix - in practice is the Polling District (e.g. “PD1”) | YES  |
|  Elector No. | Elector Number (e.g. “136”) | YES  |
|  Elector No. Suffix | Elector Number Suffix (e.g. “0”) | YES  |
|  Full Elector No. | Full Elector Number (e.g. “PD1-136-0”)
Note that this must use “-” as the separator (not e.g. “_”) |   |
|  Forename | Elector Forename (e.g. “John”) | YES  |
|  Middle Names | Elector Middle Names (e.g. “Michael”)
Note that this is not displayed within the system |   |
|  Surname | Elector Surname (e.g. “Smith”) | YES  |
|  Date of Attainment | The date at which the individual has reached, or is soon to reach, the age at which they can vote
Note that
• This is not their date of birth, and if the Electoral Register contains that, it needs to be first converted into a date of attainment
• If an invalid value is entered into this field, the upload will fail (i.e. the field has to either be blank or have a valid date) |   |

©TTW Digital Limited 2025

6

TTW Digital

# Electoral Register fields (2) Address

The Data File can have the following fields for each Elector’s address

|  Data File Column | Definition | Required?  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  Address1 | Mapped to the following address fields within the system
• House Name | Yes  |
|  Address2 | • House Number | Yes  |
|  Address3 | • Road Name
You must have House Name and/or House Number |   |
|  Address4-6 | Enabled, but currently other address information (e.g. town, borough etc.) is not imported |   |
|  PostCode | Post Code | Yes  |
|  UPRN | Unique Property Reference Number is the unique identifier for every addressable location across the UK, e.g. the UPRN for 10 Downing Street is 100023336956
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unique_Property_Reference_Number
Note that this data may be used in future releases |   |

- All addresses will load correctly if they are in one of the supported formats - this will be the case for most records in most Electoral Registers
- Addresses with Building Names in particular need checking to ensure they are in a supported format
- Further details on supported formats are given in the following slides
- Lambeth Green Party has developed an address preparation tool that you may wish to use - please contact pedrojuk@yahoo.co.uk for more details
- It is not necessary to get every address correct in the initial upload, as Local Admin Users can correct an address as and when an issue is spotted: see User Guide <to be="" added="">

©TTW Digital Limited 2025

TTW Digital</to>

# House Number and Road Name (1)

If there is no House Name, then a numeric House Number and a Road Name will be correctly interpreted provided they are in either

- Address1 (House Number then Road Name)
- Address1 (House Number) and Address2 (Road Name)

This covers the vast majority of addresses and so no manual intervention will be required

Examples - Valid

|  Data File Field
Address 1 | Data File Field
Address 2 | Data File Field
Address 3 | System Field
House Number | System Field
House Name | System Field
Road Name  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  8 Coleman Road | - | - | 8 | - | Coleman Road  |
|  8 | Coleman Road | - | 8 | - | Coleman Road  |

Example - Invalid

|  Data File Field
Address 1 | Data File Field
Address 2 | Data File Field
Address 3 | System Field
House Number | System Field
House Name | System Field
Road Name  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  8 | - | Coleman Road | 8 | 8 | Coleman Road  |

©TTW Digital Limited 2025

TTW Digital

# House Number and Road Name (2)

If there is no House Name, then a mixed alphanumeric House Number and a Road Name will be correctly interpreted provided they are in either

- Address1 (House Number then Road Name)
- Address1 (House Number) and Address2 (Road Name)

Examples - Valid

|  Data File Field
Address 1 | Data File Field
Address 2 | Data File Field
Address 3 | System Field
House Number | System Field
House Name | System Field
Road Name  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  16A Coleman Road | - | - | 16 | 16A | Coleman Road  |
|  16B | Coleman Road | - | 16 | 16B | Coleman Road  |

Example - Invalid

|  Data File Field
Address 1 | Data File Field
Address 2 | Data File Field
Address 3 | System Field
House Number | System Field
House Name | System Field
Road Name  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  16C | - | Coleman Road | 16C | 16C | Coleman Road  |

©TTW Digital Limited 2025

TTW Digital

# Building Names (1) Overview

Building Names are more complex - if there is a Building Name (this also covers a flat address e.g. "Basement Flat") then

- Building Name must be on its own in Address1
- “Ontario Point”
- “Basement Flat”

- A number or a letter can be included in Address1 provided it is after the name
- “Flat 1”
- “Flat B”
- “Ontario Point Flat 1”
- “Ontario Point 1”
- “Ontario Point Flat B”
- “Ontario Point B”
- “[100-102]”

- House Number and Road Name must follow in either
- Address2 (House Number then Road Name)
- Address2 (House Number) and Address3 (Road Name)

- If there is no House Number, then Road Name must follow in Address2
- House Number will be recorded as “0” (zero)

©TTW Digital Limited 2025

TTW Digital

|  B | 16 Coleman Road | - | 16 | B | Coleman Road  |

|  James Cottage | 52a Coleman Road |  | 52 | James Cottage 52a | Coleman Road  |
|  James Cottage 52a | 52 Coleman Road |  | 52 | James Cottage 52a | Coleman Road  |

|  Data File Field Address 1 | Data File Field Address 2 | Data File Field Address 3 | System Field House Number | System Field House Name | System Field Road Name  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  14 South House | Coleman Road | - | 14 | - | South House  |

# Building Names (4) Recommended

To make Rounds set up easy, the recommended address format is as follows

|  Data File Field Address 1 | Data File Field Address 2 | Data File Field Address 3 | System Field House Number | System Field House Name | System Field Road Name  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  <BUILDING> xxx | <ROAD NAME> | → | 0 | <BUILDING> xxx | <STREET NAME>  |
|  <BUILDING> xxx | y <ROAD NAME> | → | y | <BUILDING> xxx | <STREET NAME>  |

- “xxx” is the flat number using as many preceding zeroes as necessary
- If a block has 150 addresses, then it should be 001 (first address) 010 100 150 (final address)
- “y” is the (optional) street number of the building and if blank, is treated as 0

©TTW Digital Limited 2025

TTW Digital

# Data Preparation - Pre-Existing Data

©TTW Digital Limited 2025

14

TTW Digital

# Pre-Existing Data fields (1)

You can upload data for up to 10 previous or future elections relating to electors in the current Electoral Register

> Note that each Election must be set up in the system as per “Elections” in [User Guide: C1 Party Management](User Guide: C1 Party Management)

For every election, you can upload the following

|  CSV Column | Definition | Valid Values  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  <ELECTION NAME> Green Voting Intention | Likelihood of voting Green | 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, BLANK  |
|  <ELECTION NAME> Party | Usual party | See following slide  |

For a historic election, you can additionally upload

|  CSV Column | Definition | Valid Values  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  <ELECTION NAME> Voted | Whether the elector voted in that election | Any value = “Y”
BLANK = “N”  |

For a future election, you can additionally upload

|  CSV Column | Definition | Valid Values  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  <ELECTION NAME> Postal Voter | If the elector has a postal vote | Any value = “Y”
BLANK = “N”  |

©TTW Digital Limited 2025

15

TTW Digital

# Pre-Existing Data fields (2)

You can upload data for the following Usual Parties
- Greens
- Labour
- Conservatives
- Liberal Democrats
- Reform (and its predecessors)
- Plaid Cymru
- Residents Association
- Independent
- Others

Ensure you use a consistent coding method, e.g.
- “G” means “Green Party”
- “L” means “Labour”
- “LD” means “LibDems”
- “Ind” means “Independent”
etc.

©TTW Digital Limited 2025
16
TTW Digital

# Preparing the Data File

©TTW Digital Limited 2025

17

TTW Digital

# File format (1)

Use one of the templates
- [Data File Template - Electoral Register Only](Data File Template - Electoral Register Only)
- [Data File Template - Electoral Register plus Data](Data File Template - Electoral Register plus Data)

or ensure that your data file use these field (column) names (see slides 4, 5 and11)

Confirm the format
- Ensure that all required fields (columns) have the correct field name (column header) in Row 1
- Delete any optional fields (columns) for which you do not have data
- Ensure that your file has field names (column headers) that are unique

Check the data fields
- Ensure that all required fields (columns) are populated
- Ensure that optional fields (columns) are populated where data exists
- Ensure that fields (columns) have valid data
- Some electors may have no address (e.g. for security reasons) - ensure these electors have been deleted
- Specifically check Building Names (see slides 8 and 9)

Example Data Files (populated with test data)
- [Electoral Register Only](Electoral Register Only)
- [Electoral Register plus Data](Electoral Register plus Data) for one historic Election and one future Election

©TTW Digital Limited 2025

TTW Digital

# File format (2)

The upload file needs to be saved in a CSV UTF-8 (Comma-Separated Values) format

## Excel files

![img-0.jpeg](img-0.jpeg)

> Note that there are other CSV variants available in Excel - use of those can cause the upload to fail

## Google Sheets

![img-1.jpeg](img-1.jpeg)

# Upload

©TTW Digital Limited 2025

20

TTW Digital

# Submit to TTW Support for Upload

First parties to onboard, we will directly support you with the set up and load into our Sandbox environment

- Check that the data uploads correctly
- Check you can see the uploaded data in the Canvassing Screens as expected
- Ask us any questions about the data load and raise any issues

Parties onboarding later will be able to check this directly in the Sandbox environment

- This User Guide will be updated at that point to step through the data upload process
